steps.title: Interpret the time span
steps.content: The equation represents the population after $10$ years, from January $1$, $2005$ to January $1$, $2015$. The starting population is $31{,}480$, and the ending population is $32{,}210$. The added term $10x$ must represent the total increase over those $10$ years.

steps.title: Connect the multiplier
steps.content: Because $x$ is multiplied by $10$ years, $x$ represents the amount of increase for one year, not the total increase. The equation would give $$10x=32{,}210-31{,}480$$ so $x$ is the total change divided evenly across the years. That means $x$ is the average yearly increase in the town population. The answer is B.
